key_insight: VRR-Stop decides when to stop repairing an LLM’s answer by estimating whether the next verify-repair step has positive true value, even when both verification and repair are noisy.
engineering_relevance: This gives LLM agent builders a principled stopping rule that can reduce unnecessary repairs, avoid self-damaging edits, and improve reliability in code, math, and tool-use workflows.
method: Uses a four-parameter noise model to separate verifier false accepts/rejects from repairer help/damage behavior.
Aggregates repeated verification votes with belief filtering to estimate committed validity.
Adds VRR-Guard, a fallback rule that stops relying on estimation and uses a verification-margin threshold when calibration becomes unreliable.
evidence_signal: On a GSM8K stress test, VRR-Stop improved final true validity by 60.6 percentage points over fixed five-round repair while averaging only 0.72 repair rounds.
watch_item: The method depends on verifier discrimination and a sufficient decision margin; when discrimination is near zero, estimation error can flip the stopping decision, making the fallback safeguard important.
paper_title: Verify, Repair, Repeat, or Stop? Robust Stopping for Noisy Verify-Repair Loops in LLM Agents
